title: Where the Roads Learn to Sail

features.name: the dissolved bitumen
features.desc-on-touch: A road that has begun to accept water instead of refuse it. The asphalt still holds the memory of traffic, but now fish swim through those ruts. Tyre-marks have become current-channels.

features.name: the eucalyptus navigation marker
features.desc-on-touch: A tree that has learned the roads' route and translates it into wood-patterns. Its bark reads like a chart. Its branches point to places the old roads used to lead. The tree is ancient; the roads forgot they ever existed much earlier.

features.name: the seagull's impossible flight
features.desc-on-touch: A creature rendered in seven grey polygons, circling as if it remembers the road as a thing-to-eat. It speaks in archive signals: beep, chirp, beep. The corruption has given it the memory of asphalt and rubber. It can no longer land.

features.name: the sailboat resting-place
features.desc-on-touch: A small wooden structure, half-shed half-keel. It has the shape of both building and vessel. It waits for the water to rise enough that it need not choose which it wants to be.

inscription: the roads grew tired of holding still; they learned the ocean's grammar; now they are both ground and journey

file.name: Navigation_Map_Waterlogged_Date_Unknown.txt
file.note: Once a street directory for a town that is slowly learning to swim. Routes now loop through water. Destinations have become tides. The last annotation reads: 'if you follow these roads now, you will a

exits.label: back toward the shore where the broadcasts fail
into the estuary where rivers learn their old names
toward the ocean's horizon, where maps finally surrender
